title: cupania scrobiculata
intro: Cupania scrobiculata, also known as the roughcup tree, is a species of plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cupania scrobiculata include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are made by boiling the roots, leaves, or bark in water to extract its active compounds.
